issue_title: Nested tables should be written below their parent table
issue_body: Though not a requirement of the spec, it would be a very nice feature of this library to, when encoding nested dictionaries, write nested tables directly below their parent table, instead of first all top-level sections then the next level, then the next.

issue_title: Content-Disposition get urlencoded if filename is unicode string
issue_body:
Content-Disposition get urlencoded when storing file with unicode name to S3 backend. Content-Disposition looks like 'inline;%20test.txt'. As a result, browser is unable to parse filename from it.
See test: https://github.com/eprikazc/depot/blob/urlencoded_content_disposition/tests/test_awss3_storage.py#L70

P.S.
I tried fixing it by setting header directly, as suggested here - https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/boto-users/17M3eRUX0_0
However it does not seem to work - the value still urlencoded.

We can try checking if boto3 has the same issue or not.
UPDATE: Tried with boto3 and the issue is not there. So, looks like we need to do 2 things here:

1. Build content-disposition according to rfc6266 (as described in the link above). rfc6266 takes care of properly encoding filename. Like:
>>> rfc6266.build_header(u'привет.txt', 'inline')                                                                                                     
"inline; filename*=utf-8''%D0%BF%D1%80%D0%B8%D0%B2%D0%B5%D1%82.txt"

2. Switch to boto3 to get rid of url encoding.

@amol- - what do you think? Shall I proceed with implementation?

issue_title: New rule for path completion
issue_body:
I use a utility which auto-completes file paths in my bash commands. For ex:
```
$ cd thefuck
Executing cd ~/gitrepos/forked/thefuck
```
It runs `cd` initially. If it fails, it catches the file name due to which it failed from the error message, substitutes it with complete path and run again.

If it is integrated in `thefuck`, it would look something like:
```
$ cd thefuck
bash: cd: thefuck: No such file or directory
$ fuck
# Execute: cd ~/gitrepos/forked/thefuck
```
It is not limited to `cd`. Any command that takes files / dirs as arguments are aliased with this utility as wrapper. Fuck version of those would look like:
```
$ mv thefuck .
mv: cannot stat 'thefuck': No such file or directory
$ fuck
# Execute: mv ~/gitrepos/forked/thefuck .
```
(Path completion is done on basis of history and locate output)

I use both, thefuck as well as my utility and was looking to integrate them.  I have added a quick test [here](https://github.com/luviiit/thefuck/blob/167b82be8dd9eb96c919efe037ab0708b9a2cb20/tests/rules/test_path_complete.py).

My utility is written in bash and I would have to port it in Python. Would love to know your opinion on this before proceeding.

issue_title: internal_roster can become a stale cache; no way to reset
issue_body:
The redis entry "will_roster" is persisted and holds a mapping of hipchat ids to often-accessed user data. It's initialized first in update_will_roster_and_rooms() and the user data within is accessed very many times.

Each subsequent update_will_roster_and_rooms() is additive and will not overwrite any user data that already exists (i.e. 'if user is not "": ..." and "if not hasattr nick: ...").

**When a user changes their name/nick/mention_name, there is no mechanism to push that change into the "will_roster" cache**, so their user data (from will's POV) will continue to be their old user data.

This certainly bit me writing a plugin that **expected message.sender.nicks to accurately represent their current nick, not the first nick that the will plugin ever saw them use**.

In order of the strength of changes:
- Why is this cache persisted to disk and not just held in memory?
- Assuming it's better/easier to leave it persisted, can it clear the cache on startup? (self.save("will_roster", {}) during boot path somewhere.)
- Assuming that hack is too ugly, can update_will_roster...() check for discrepancies in the cache on each invocation instead of giving up when it checks for existence?
- Assuming this behaviour is intentional or otherwise too ugly to fix, can a ("SERIOUSLY!...") admin-y access be added that drops this cache and forces it to be regenerated?

I have a temporary workaround in the form of hand-editing the "will_roster" on any nick changes (carefully, dotting i's, crossing t's), though it's less than desirable.

issue_title: Use templating engine for generated files
issue_body:
Currently we put the templates of the generated project files (e.g. the TCL file for Vivado) inside the backend code itself. That's hard to maintain and annoying to modify.

How about using a templating engine like Jinja for that instead? I'll come up with a patch if you don't have fundamental issues with that @olofk.

In the long run, this would also enable us to let users specify their own template, which is then used and filled by fusesoc. [However, that's optional future stuff, nothing I'm thinking of implementing right now.]

issue_title: Need type instance test variables
issue_body:
The idiom:

```python
tripleExpr = Union["EachOf", "OneOf", "TripleConstraint", tripleExprLabel]
if isinstance(x, tripleExpr):
       ...
```
Doesn't work, as tripleExpr is a type.  We need some mechanism to accommodate this requirement.

One possibility would be to add:
```python
tripleExprType_ = (EachOf, OneOf, TripleConstraint, tripleExprLabelType_)
```
at the end of the generation, although there will be issues with recursive references to be resolved.
We could then say:
```python
if isinstance(x, tripleExprType_)
         ...
```

A second option would be to extend the typing library to implement the desired behavior directly.

Yet another option would be to create a "typeOf" function:

```python
if isinstance(x, typeOf(tripleExpr):
        ...
```

issue_title: Issue with regular expressions
issue_body:
Hello.

I have an issue with the matches statement in the Ruby implementation. Here is the code:

```
Durable.ruleset :split_string do
  when_all (m.subject.matches("/.*?(\d{2}-?\w{3}-?\d{2}\/)?-?(\w{3,6}\*?)(-\d{8})?-(\w{4})(-\d{4})?-(\w{1}).*/")) do
    splitted = m.subject.split("//").select{|segment| segment.match(/.*?(\d{2}-?\w{3}-?\d{2}\/)?-?(\w{3,6}\*?)(-\d{8})?-(\w{4})(-\d{4})?-(\w{1})/)}
    i=1
    splitted.each do |segment|
      post :process_string,{:id=>m.id, :sid=>m.sid.to_s+i.to_s, :segment=>segment}
      i=i+1
    end
  end
  when_start do
    post :split_string, {:id=>1, :sid=>2, :subject =>"AAA/CV/KK:01ENE01//ABCCDE*-CVCV-X//GTY-CVCV-X//XYZ-CVCV-X//TRE-CVCV-X//HUK*-CVCV-X//ABC-CVCV-U//N"}
  end
end

Durable.ruleset :process_string do
  when_all (m.segment !="") do
    puts "segment => #{m.segment}"
  end
end

Durable.run_all
```

The regular expression at the when_all statement in the :split_string rule is working regularly in ruby but not in here, actually, the application stalls when the rules are being loaded.

Is there a way to use regular expressions? Or, if it's something else, it would be nice to have it in the documentation.

I'm using

issue_title: PicketFence does not detect pickets for small patterns
issue_body: Pickets are derived from a median profile along each image axis, but if the picket fence pattern itself is smaller than half the image (e.g. a large SSD) then the median will be zero and not detect the pickets. Behavior should take the maximum instead.

issue_title: does sshtunnel allow local port forwarded to remote host?
issue_body:
Hi,

I would like to use sshtunnel for a fully python implementation of these shell commands: 
1. `ssh –L 9999:127.0.0.1:8888 user@server` so I can use server:8888 service on my local:9999
2. `ssh –R 7777:127.0.0.1:6666 user@server` so server can use service in local:6666 internally, as server:7777 

The aim to use sshtunnel is to have this feature both on windows and linux without adding extra packet.
The first point is correctly satisfied by this:

```python
from sshtunnel import SSHTunnelForwarder

###   ssh –L 9999:127.0.0.1:8888 user@server

server = SSHTunnelForwarder(
    '10.0.0.1', #server host
    ssh_username="user",
    ssh_password="pass",
    remote_bind_address=('127.0.0.1', 8888),
    local_bind_address=('127.0.0.1', 9999)
)

server.start()

print(server.local_bind_port)  # show assigned local port
# work with `SECRET SERVICE` through `server.local_bind_port`.

server.stop()
```

I would like to ask if there is a simple way to obtain also the second point.
I've tried to change configuration parameters but with no success. I've also tried to use paramiko library but seems to not work on windows as sshtunnel does.

Thanks in advance

Periodic triggers created with win32com.taskscheduler are always disabled
issue_body: In creating periodic tasks using win32com.taskscheduler, (at least on Windows 10 and WS2012), the "status" field of triggers always shows as "Disabled," and, without the trigger being enabled, the task never actually fires.  We've tried this with the latest version of pywin32 2.7, but it still doesn't work, and manual intervention (editing the task via the task scheduler) is required to actually enable the trigger.  In reviewing Microsoft's documentation, it's not clear that this bit is even exposed in the taskscheduler 1.0 API, but it is exposed in the ITrigger interface in the 2.0 API (from Vista and WS2008 onward.)

issue_title: using quaternion.quaternion on a numpy array
issue_body:
Hello,
Along my way to find a method to apply the same rotation to an array of 3D points (x,y,z coordinates), I've experimented with the constructor `quaternion.quaternion(0,*one_by_3_np_array)`.
In order to be able to directly construct a "pure" quaternion (no real part) from my array of 3D points, I thought I could do:
```
# define my test XYZ array
myxyz = np.random.rand(100,3)
myxyz_quat = quaternion.quaternion(0,*myxyz) 
```
But found out that obviously this doesn't work because unpacking my array will result in more than the 4 floats expected by the constructor.
I can define a function to build my quaternion line by line but doubt this would be efficient.
The solution I've came up with is to use `quaternion.as_quat_array`:
```
def npXYZary_to_quaternion (npary):
    tmpary = np.zeros((npary.shape[0],4))
    tmpary[:,1:] = npary
    qnpary = quaternion.as_quat_array(tmpary)
    return qnpary
```
But could it be possible to have the quaternion constructor take a Nx3 (Nx4 as well) numpy array ?
Ludovic

issue_title: Improve Event Listener
issue_body:
A big problem with the Event Listener is that the socketio client libraries for Python all basically suck. I did, however, find a Websocket library that doesn't suck... so I've written a very light weight EngineIO/SocketIO client that sits on top of this Websocket library. Below are the notes I've compiled for this EngineIO/SocketIO code:

_The SocketIO protocol is in fact two protocols. The "transport" is EngineIO and the messaging protocol is SocketIO._

**EngineIO Protocol**
  `<packet type id>[<data>]`
  Ex: 0{"sid":"kOeJt3LgKj2im3UVAFiO","upgrades":[],"pingInterval":25000,"pingTimeout":60000}
  Ex: 2probe
  Ex: 3probe
  Ex: 2
  Ex: 3
  Ex: 40
  Ex: 42["com.goabode.device.update","ZW:00000005"]

**Packet Types:**

0 open
  Sent from the server when a new transport is opened with JSON-encoded handshake data:
      sid                 session ID
      upgrades            possible transport upgrades
      pingTimeout         server configured ping timeout - Time to wait for pong response from server before disconnecting
      pingInterval        server configured ping interval - Time between client sends ping packets

1 close
  Request to close the polling interface since polling will open and close sockets regularly. Not required for websockets but will still react to just in case.

2 ping
  Sent by the client to the server. Should be sent every pingInterval.

3 pong
  Sent by the server to the client to respond to a ping. Should be received within pingTimeout after the last ping.

4 message
  Messages to or from the server. We only care about recieving data.

5 upgrade
  Upgrade test from one transport to another. Since we're only using websockets we can ignore this.

6 noop
  A "no op" packet. Ignored.

**Ping Timeouts**
  The client must send a ping packet every pingInteveral. The client will consider the server unresponsive after not recieving a
  reply from the server for pingTimeout. Since the two values are shared between the server and the client, the server
  will also be able to detect whether the client becomes unresponsive when it does not receive any data within pingTimeout + pingInterval

**SocketIO Protocol**
  The data portion of an EngineIO packet type 4 will utilize the SocketIO protocol.
  Ex: 40
  Ex: 42["com.goabode.device.update","ZW:00000005"]
  Ex: 42["com.goabode.gateway.timeline",{"mac":"B0:C5:CA:30:0A:C8","id":213014558,"date":"04/03/2018","time":"01:15 PM","event_utc":1522786544,"event_cid":"","event_code":"5125","device_id":"ZW:00000005","device_type_id":"66","device_type":"Dimmer","nest_has_motion":"","nest_has_sound":"","nest_has_person":"","device_name":"Kitchen Lights","file_path":"","deep_link":"","file_name":"","file_size":"","file_count":"","file_is_del":0,"event_type":"On","severity":"8","pos":"l","color":"
000000","is_alarm":"0","icon":"assets/email/lightbulb-on.png","user_id":"","user_name":"","mobile_name":"","parent_tid":"","app_type":"","viewed_by_uid":"","verified_by_tid":"","event_name":"Kitchen Lights On","event_by":""}]

  In the above examples the first '4' is the EngineIO message type and everything after it is the SocketIO message type.

  The SocketIO message itself if some sort of format like: <packet type><message type><optional namespace><optional event ID>[data] (ex: 42/chat,[“join”,”{room:1}”])
  where optional namespace is "/chat", defaulting to /, and ID is used for the ack. The data portion is essentially a json array where the first index is a string for the event name
  and the second index onward can be a string or json object.

  We will dumb this down: When we receive an event we will parse out only the [data] portion and parse it as json into a dict, pull out the event_name, and then pass that along with
  the event data through to an on_event method.

**Message Types:**

0 connect         Confirms SocketIO Message Connection
1 disconnect      Transmitted before SocketIO Disconnect? Not sure where this is used but will similarly react.
2 event           Event message - What We've Done This For
3 ack             Acknowledge an event - Ignored
4 error           Error message (for example, authentication failure).
5 binary event    Binary data event - Ignored for our purposes currently
6 binary ack      Acknowledge a binary event - Ignored

https://github.com/wildfoundry/dataplicity-lomond/
https://www.willmcgugan.com/blog/tech/post/announcing-lomond/
http://lomond.readthedocs.io/en/latest/
https://github.com/socketio/engine.io-protocol
https://github.com/socketio/socket.io-protocol

issue_title: remove_watch functionality
issue_body:
Few points regarding remove_watch functionality.
1. remove_watch should propagate superficial argument to remove_watch_with_id (but I see #57 is already in place for that)
- for this this the Tree-Handling would need probably a fix (use superficial only for currently unhandeled but probably wanted DELETE event #58 / #51 but not for MOVED_FROM as this does not unregister the watch from inotify see also #46 for a possible result...)
2. I would like a public method to remove a wd only from underlying inotify (inotify.calls.inotify_rm_watch) to have possibility to remove the watch from one thread and watching thread is propagating the change which is does not using public remove_watch as it suppress the event if diretory name cannot be looked up) - possibly just a parameter (why not reuse superficial so True = only tracking information, False = tracking information + watch, None = watch only...)
3. method remove_watch_with_id should not be public (without _) in current form as the self.__watches is not cleanup up using this method and there is no public method to only cleanup that - but best to make both existing methods removing both forward and reverse lookup entries for wd (and if superficial is False also remove the wd from underlying inotify)
4. think about handling IN_IGNORE events received (in this case the the wd was unregistered automatically by underlying inotify) - so IN_IGNORE could cause automatic remove_watch with superficial set to True (sure that could be left up to the user. but at least there should be working methods for this case, currently is neighter - one does not support superficial the other does not remove forward entry from map..) - anyway if this would be implemented this could break the 

Btw. in _BaseTree event_gen is another bug... The IN_MOVED_TO is handled twice so it will log a warning in that case...

issue_title: Heuristic based title detection
issue_body:
Hi, I saw that you perform PDF table detection using an algorithm from the Nuriminen thesis.

Just like tables, one of the things that you can extract from a PDF is its title.

Some PDFs have the "title" metadata file, but more often than not, they contain wrong text.

KDEFileMetadata (written in C++) is one of the projects that used some heuristics to guess the title. [You can have a look at the code here.](https://github.com/KDE/kfilemetadata/commit/1491c4a2d9fd7adb075258d7eb65f4ae1d7169f4#diff-ec6bd17e68486d574e420abfcfffbc88)

A feature like this is useful in detecting title from research papers - which could further be used to rename the PDF files! 

So from a filename like `gfs-sosp2003.pdf` you can get `Google File System - 2003.pdf`

issue_title: Pyfolio data 1 min
.resampledata() from faster timeframe of IB data never shows up.
issue_body:
@mementum not sure if you are looking at this, but have modified your test script to investigate the issue with `.resampledata()` seeming to never become available. End result is that any system attempting to resample from a faster timeframe never gets these bars.

Here is the modified script. It demonstrates that `Data2` is never seen.

```python
from __future__ import (absolute_import, division, print_function,
                        unicode_literals)

import datetime
import backtrader as bt


class TestStrategy(bt.Strategy):
    def __init__(self):
        self.datastatus = False

    def notify_data(self, data, status, *args, **kwargs):
        print('*' * 5, 'DATA NOTIF: %s' % data._name,
              data._getstatusname(status), *args)

        if status == data.LIVE:
            self.datastatus = True

    def notify_store(self, msg, *args, **kwargs):
        print('*' * 5, 'STORE NOTIF:', msg)

    def prenext(self):
        self.next()

    def next(self):
        if not self.datastatus:
            print("datastatus: %s" % self.datastatus)
            return

        print('-- %004d' % len(self), str(self.datetime.datetime()))
        for i, d in enumerate(di for di in self.datas if len(di)):
            out = ['Data%d' % i, d._name, '%004d' % len(d),
                   str(d.datetime.datetime()), str(d.close[0])]

            print(','.join(out))


if __name__ == '__main__':
    TICKER0, SSEND0, TZ0 = 'ES-201703-GLOBEX-USD', datetime.time(15, 15), 'CST6CDT'
    TICKER1, SSEND1, TZ1 = 'SPY-STK-SMART-USD', datetime.time(16, 0), 'EST5EDT'

    NAME0, NAME1 = TICKER0.split('-')[0], TICKER1.split('-')[0]

    cerebro = bt.Cerebro()

    storekwargs = dict(host='127.0.4.1', port=7465, clientId=93,)
    ibstore = bt.stores.IBStore(**storekwargs)
    data0 = ibstore.getdata(dataname=TICKER0, sessionend=SSEND0, name=NAME0, tz=TZ0)
    cerebro.resampledata(data0, timeframe=bt.TimeFrame.Minutes, compression=1)

    data1 = ibstore.getdata(dataname=TICKER1, sessionend=SSEND1, name=NAME1, tz=TZ1)
    cerebro.resampledata(data1, timeframe=bt.TimeFrame.Days, compression=1)

    # should see the following as Data2
    cerebro.resampledata(data0, timeframe=bt.TimeFrame.Days, compression=1, name='ES-day')

    cerebro.addstrategy(TestStrategy)
    cerebro.run(runonce=False, tradehistory=True, exactbars=0)
```

issue_title: ia upload from stdin accepts data faster than it uploads
issue_body:
Consider a script like the following:
```
tar -cv 100MegabyteFolder | pv -ps "100M" | ia upload identifier - --remote-name="100MegabyteFolder.tar"
```
The progress bar shown by `pv` will be wrong (it will indicate completion prematurely) when uploading using a slow Internet connection, because ia upload accepts the input from stdin faster than it uploads.

issue_title: Tasks with autoretry_for stuck in locked state
issue_body:
I've discovered a bug in `get_key` method which prevents lock release.
```
@app.task(name='mytask', base=celery_once.QueueOnce)
def mytask(foo, bar):
    ...

mytask.delay(foo=1, bar=2)
```
The `getcallargs(self.run, *args, **kwargs)` call returns `{'foo': 1, 'bar': 2}`. 
The `get_key` call returns `qo_mytask_foo-1_bar-2`. Everything works properly.

If I'd add `autoretry_for` argument:
```
task(name='mytask_retry', base=celery_once.QueueOnce, autoretry_for=(Exception, ))
def mytask_retry(foo, bar):
    ...

mytask_retry.delay(foo=1, bar=2)
```

The `getcallargs(self.run, *args, **kwargs)` call returns `{'args': (), 'kwargs': {'foo': 1, 'bar': 2}}`. 
The `get_key` call returns `qo_mytask_retry_args-()_kwargs={'bar': 2, 'foo': 1}`. 
The `{'bar': 2, 'foo': 1}` part is unsorted and sometimes the order changes to  `{'foo': 1, 'bar': 2}`. 

The same issue takes place when a nested dict is passed as task's argument. For example, `mytask(foo={'spam': 10, 'eggs': 20}, bar=2)`. The `get_key` call returns `qo_mytask_foo-{'eggs': 20, 'spam': 10}_bar-2` and sometimes `qo_mytask_foo-{'spam': 10, 'eggs': 20}_bar-2`


This lead to lock release issues because `get_key` result is different on lock stage and release stage.

issue_title: Suggestion for re-initialize proxy after CCU was down.
issue_body:
Hi,
if I restart CCu2  without restart HASS, I need to reconnect Homematic `Component` to get Write access vie RPC again.

Log tells: WARNING (Thread-8) [pyhomematic._hm] ServerThread.jsonRpcLogin: Unable to open session.

If the exception keep uncaught, pyhomematic Component stops to work (no longer seen at log)

this can be fixed by the following change : "self.proxyInit()" from snippet below in _hm.py

```
    def jsonRpcLogin(self, remote):
        """Login to CCU and return session"""
        session = False
        try:
            params = {"username": self.remotes[remote][
                'username'], "password": self.remotes[remote]['password']}
            response = self._rpcfunctions.jsonRpcPost(
                self.remotes[remote]['ip'], "Session.login", params)
            if response['error'] is None and response['result']:
                session = response['result']

            if not session:
                LOG.warning(
                    "ServerThread.jsonRpcLogin: Unable to open session.")
                self.proxyInit()
        except Exception as err:
            LOG.debug(
                "ServerThread.jsonRpcLogin: Exception while logging in via JSON-RPC: %s" % str(err))
        return session
```

issue_title: Visible grouping of widgets
issue_body:
See https://github.com/peterbrittain/asciimatics/issues/103 for context.  This issue tracks the request to add more visual cues to the grouping of widgets.

The simplest use case is just to allow for a clear division between columns in a Layout, so that widgets in one don't touch the widgets in another - e.g. by having a vertical divider between each.

The more complex case is to provide a box around any arbitrary set of widgets.  To keep things sane on the automatic placement logic, I think this will need to be restricted to widgets in the same column, or, at the very least, in the same Layout.

issue_title: int(s, 16)
issue_body:
I tried Pythran on the Julia benchmarks https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/master/test/perf/micro/perf.py

One of the function can not be compiled by Pythran because the second argument of the function int() is not implemented (see https://github.com/serge-sans-paille/pythran/blob/master/pythran/pythonic/__builtin__/int_.hpp).

The function:
```python
from random import randint

# pythran export parse_int(int)

def parse_int(t):
    for i in range(1, t):
        n = randint(0, 2**32-1)
        s = hex(n)
        if s.endswith('L'):
            s = s[:-1]
        m = int(s, 16)
        assert m == n
    return n

```
[only_parse_int.txt](https://github.com/serge-sans-paille/pythran/files/1608012/only_parse_int.txt)

issue_title: Setting A Query Timeout
issue_body:
Based on this post by the author: https://github.com/lionheart/django-pyodbc/issues/18 the connection timeout can be set after the connection is established and this can act as a 'Query Timeout'. 

I have tested this myself with the following snippet:

`import pyodbc
import time

start = time.time()
try:
    conn = pyodbc.connect(
        "DRIVER=/usr/lib64/libtdsodbc.so.0;"
        "SERVER=10.10.10.10;"
        "PORT=1433;"
        "DATABASE=DEV_SERVER;"
        "UID=user;"
        "PWD=password;"
        "TDS_Version=7.3;", timeout=1)
    conn.timeout = 1
    cursor = conn.cursor()
    cursor.execute("WAITFOR DELAY '00:00:05'")
except:
    raise
finally:
    stop = time.time()
    print(stop - start)
`
Knowing this, is there a reason something like this was not implemented in this library? 

I am thinking of working on a PR for a 'query_timeout'. My testing has not showed any issue with this so far.

issue_title: Allow for subselects
issue_body:
Hi, 

I've come across an issue with subselects. 

My first problem was that i couldn't use the result of a `_select`  in an equals comparison operator, later i found out i can use `belongs` for that. Now i don't know if that will have performance penalties on some platforms, but i consider it for later concern. 

First and foremost, i need to combine two select statements that are related. 

Consider  the following model: 
```python 
db = DAL('sqlite:memory')
db.define_table('data',Field('effdt','datetime'),Field('v'), Field('s'))
db.data.insert(effdt=datetime.now(),v=10, s='mine')
db.data.insert(effdt=datetime.now()-timedelta(5),v=5,s='mine')
db.data.insert(effdt=datetime.now()+timedelta(5),v=15,s='mine')
db.data.insert(effdt=datetime.now(),v=10, s='other')
db.data.insert(effdt=datetime.now()-timedelta(5),v=5,s='other')
db.data.insert(effdt=datetime.now()+timedelta(5),v=15,s='other')
```

Now when i try to get the current v for both 'mine' and 'other'  i use the following: 

```python
print('- #0 ----------')
print (db.executesql("""
select effdt, v, s
from data d
where effdt == (
  select max(d_ed.effdt)
  from data d_ed
  where d_ed.effdt <= $NOW
    and d_ed.s = d.s
)
""",dict(NOW=datetime.now())))
```

I've tried to move this into DAL code, and have been struggling with it for quite some time. 
Now i came up with this: 

```python
d = db.data.with_alias('d')
d_ed = db.data.with_alias('d_ed')
subselect = db((d_ed.effdt <= datetime.now()) & (d_ed.s == d.s))._select(d_ed.effdt.max())
result = db(d.effdt.belongs(subselect.replace(', data AS d',''))).select(d.effdt, d.v, d.s)
```
which fortunately produces accurate results. 
But i find it quite ugly to patch the subselect string with data that is quite 'woven' with the structure of the query. It's not very 'dynamic'. 

I've tried alternate ways, like using an inner join on a query , but sqlite3 won't allow me to subselect with multiple columns apparently so that was one was off. 

So finally i came up with a little patch, shown below, to allow for an extra parameter to `_select`. Here's a sample for it in use: 

```python
d = db.data.with_alias('d')
d_ed = db.data.with_alias('d_ed')
subselect = db((d_ed.effdt <= datetime.now()) & (d_ed.s == d.s))._select(d_ed.effdt.max(), outer_scoped=[d])
result = db(d.effdt.belongs(subselect)).select(d.effdt, d.v, d.s)
```
So i added `outer_scoped` in which you place all tables or aliases that need not be mentioned in the `from` clause in the sql statement, because they refer to some name scoped outside of this query. Works like a charm. 

I don't have any experience with a pull request yet, but if you'd like i'll give it a try. 

Hoping for this addition to come through, cause it will save me a hell of a lot of hardcoded effective dated queries. 

Thanks. 


```diff
Index: /pydal/adapters/base.py
IDEA additional info:
Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP
<+>UTF-8
===================================================================
--- /pydal/adapters/base.py	(revision )
+++ /pydal/adapters/base.py	(revision )
@@ -39,7 +39,7 @@
 SELECT_ARGS = set(
     ('orderby', 'groupby', 'limitby', 'required', 'cache', 'left', 'distinct',
      'having', 'join', 'for_update', 'processor', 'cacheable',
-     'orderby_on_limitby'))
+     'orderby_on_limitby', 'outer_scoped'))
 
 
 class AdapterMeta(type):
@@ -1092,11 +1092,12 @@
         args_get = attributes.get
         tablenames = tables(query)
         tablenames_for_common_filters = tablenames
+        outer_scoped = [t._tablename for t in args_get('outer_scoped',[])]
         for field in fields:
             for tablename in tables(field):
                 if not tablename in tablenames:
                     tablenames.append(tablename)
@@ -1161,6 +1162,8 @@
                         if not t in important_tablenames ]
         else:
             excluded = tablenames
+
+        tablenames = [t for t in tablenames if t not in outer_scoped]
 
         if use_common_filters(query):
             query = self.common_filter(query,tablenames_for_common_filters)

```

issue_title: "is_1x1_" in convolution operator is not really used
issue_body: I found that "is_1x1_" in convolution-inl.h is not really used.  MXNet just create the "is_1x1_" but not use it to skip the im2col function call. In contrast, the "is_1x1_" in Caffe is really used to skip im2col function call to accelerate the 1x1 convolution. It is very important in some cases such as the Google's Mobilenet.

## Description

When choosing Windows as your install choice on [the install page](https://mxnet.incubator.apache.org/install/index.html), you are presented with just "validate mxnet installation" and no other content.

Master

issue_title: [FEATURE REQUEST]  trace operator
issue_body:
MXNet doesn't have an operator that calculates the trace of a matrix:
https://docs.scipy.org/doc/numpy/reference/generated/numpy.trace.html

Trace norm can be used for regularization specially with sparsity

issue_title: MXNet from Source is slower than Pip Package
issue_body:
Hi there,

I have two copies of `MXNet` - one local copy that I kept for revision and another global copy which has been installed using `sudo -H pip3 install mxnet-cu80==0.12.1`. I built my local copy using the following command:

```
make -j $(nproc) \
	USE_OPENCV=1 USE_BLAS=openblas USE_CUDA=1 \
	USE_CUDA_PATH=/usr/local/cuda USE_CUDNN=1 USE_CPP_PACKAGE=1
```

and created a virtual environment for the python binding. The problem is that - when I run the `cudnn_lstm_bucketing.py` example, what I noticed is that the local copy (throughput is around `3600 samples/sec`) is around `1000 samples/sec` slower than the global copy (throughput is around `4600 samples/sec`).

Could someone please tell me whether I am missing some build options in my `make` command? Thank you.

issue_title: Define a mx.sym.Variable which is constant in each mini-batch, but still trainable
issue_body:
Consider the following code. The **mx.sym.Variable('extra_bias')** is expected to be **[small small large small]** after training, which is indeed the case.

However, currently the inferred shape has an extra dimension (=mini-batch-size), and hence there will be mini-batch-number-of-copies of it. For example (run the code and you will see this output):
[[-0.37707356 -0.37662297  1.12921023 -0.37551349]
 [-0.37635165 -0.37655994  1.12926149 -0.37634984]
 [-0.37645847 -0.37651965  1.12986588 -0.37688756]
 [-0.37663144 -0.37624913  1.12919986 -0.37631923]
 [-0.3760089  -0.3764413   1.12953496 -0.37708497]]

How can we tell mxnet that this variable should be constant in each mini-batch (so there is no need to train such a large array), while making sure it's still trainable? Thanks.
 
```
import mxnet as mx
import logging
import math
import random
import numpy as np

# generate dummy data

n_img = 100
img_size = 2
img = [[[[ random.uniform(0, 1) for x in range(img_size) ] for y in range(img_size)] for c in range(1)] for n in range(n_img)]
label = [0 for n in range(n_img)]

for i in range(n_img):
    label[i] = 2

batch_size = 5
logging.getLogger().setLevel(logging.DEBUG)

train_iter = mx.io.NDArrayIter(data=np.array(img), label=np.array(label), batch_size=batch_size, shuffle=True)

# simple model

data = mx.sym.Variable('data')
conv = mx.sym.Convolution(data=data, kernel=(1, 1), pad=(0, 0), num_filter = 1)

# optimal value expected to be [small small large small]
extra = mx.sym.Variable('extra_bias') 

flatten = mx.sym.Flatten(data=conv)
flatten = flatten + extra
out  = mx.sym.SoftmaxOutput(data=flatten, name='softmax')

module = mx.mod.Module(symbol=out)

# training

def epoch_callback(epoch, symbol, arg_params, aux_params):
    for k in arg_params:
        print(k)
        print(arg_params[k].asnumpy())

module.fit(
    train_iter,
    eval_data = None,    
    optimizer = 'sgd',
    optimizer_params = {'learning_rate': 0.1, 'momentum': 0.0},
    num_epoch = 5,
    batch_end_callback = mx.callback.Speedometer(batch_size, 1),
    epoch_end_callback = epoch_callback
)
```

issue_title: MXNet - R API broken link
issue_body: "MXNet R Reference Manual" on http://www.mxnet.io/api/r/index.html actually contains Julia reference.

issue_title: Unmarshaling request with non-required enum fails
issue_body:
`unmarshal_request` delegates to `bravado_core.validate.validate_primitive` which fails validation when `None` is passed in for a non-required parameter.

```
>           raise error
E           ValidationError: None is not one of ['inky', 'dinky', 'doo']
E
E           Failed validating 'enum' in schema:
E               {'enum': ['inky', 'dinky', 'doo'], 'type': 'string'}
E
E           On instance:
E               None
```

I've pushed a branch with a test that captures this:

https://github.com/jacobstr/bravado-core/tree/validate-non-required-enum

issue_title: Segmentation fault and related command hangs
issue_body:
We're seeing Watchman periodically segfault on startup. Our startup sequence looks something like:
* Execute `watchman get-sockname` to get the socket name, this also starts the Watchman daemon in this case.
* Send `["watch-project", "/redactedpath/home/lkv/redactedpath/redactedpath-server"]` over the socket.
* Send `["clock", "/redactedpath/home/lkv/redactedpath/redactedpath-server"]`.

Occasionally a command hangs waiting for a response until the socket times out. The logs show a segmentation fault as below. 

We're running Ubuntu 14.04.5 LTS.

```

issue_title: '$.attr' returns undefined for boolean inputs even if they are in attribute list
issue_body:
This is pretty minor, but it's an actual bug in production for us, and I couldn't find any issues that seemed to cover this.

##### Overview

jQuery migrate causes `$.attr` to return undefined for boolean attributes if the requested boolean's property is false, even if the attribute is not. I am experiencing this problem because Firefox is incorrectly giving a checkbox input a property of `checked: false` on a live site. This is causing jQuery migrate to make `$el.attr('checked')` return `false`, although it should be returning `"checked"` because "checked=checked" is still in the attributes list.

##### Versions

This is using jQuery version 1.11.2 and jQuery migrate 1.2.1, although I believe the versions are irrelevant.

##### Simple Reproduction

Make a checkbox element that is checked, then set it's checked property to false. Then try to get its checked attribute and it will return undefined.
```
var $input = $('<input id="shippingIsBilling" name="shippingIsBilling" checked="checked" class="js-shippingIsBilling" type="checkbox">').appendTo('body')
$input.get(0).checked = false;
$input.get(0).outerHTML
// "<input id="shippingIsBilling" name="shippingIsBilling" checked="checked" class="js-shippingIsBilling" type="checkbox">"
$input.attr('checked')
// undefined
$input.prop('checked')
// false
```

##### Why it's a bug

jQuery without the migrate plugin returns "checked" for `$input.attr('checked')`. However, the logic for the migrate plugin forces boolean attributes to agree with `$.prop` in order to return true - https://github.com/jquery/jquery-migrate/blob/master/src/attributes.js#L46

##### Proposed Solution

I think that if an item is in the attributes list, it should probably return that from a `$.attr` call, instead of short-circuiting to a ( in this case, incorrect ) call to `prop`.

##### Live Reproduction

I'm only actually experiencing this problem in Firefox ( I'm on version 40.0.3, but also experienced it in 40.0.2 ). It works as expected in Chrome 44.0.2403.157.

This bug should be able to be recreated on https://www.smartdestinations.com/checkout/sdi-checkout.ep . You'll need to [add an item to your cart](http://www.smartdestinations.com/pass.ep?filters=_d_Bos_Prod_Go) first. Then, go to checkout but don't do anything. You should see 3 delivery options. Refresh the page. If all went right ( wrong? ), you should now see 5 delivery options, and can explore the bug.

You can now run the following snippets in the console:

```
$(".js-shippingIsBilling").get(0).outerHTML
// "<input id="shippingIsBilling" name="shippingIsBilling" checked="checked" class="js-shippingIsBilling" type="checkbox">"
$(".js-shippingIsBilling").attr('checked')
// undefined
$(".js-shippingIsBilling").prop('checked')
// false
```

jQuery without the migrate plugin returns "checked" for `$(".js-shippingIsBilling").attr('checked')`. I tested locally that this is true - there's not a public version of the page without the migrate plugin.

I believe this is some kind of bug in Firefox caching input values, although the input is never being set to false, so that doesn't REALLY make sense.

I think Firefox should stop doing whatever it's doing that's messing this up, and if it did, I wouldn't care about this "bug" being fixed in jQuery migrate. I'm not sure how to describe it any more clearly than this report, so I'll probably just link this in bugzilla, if it will let me.

issue_title: Incompatible with gevent monkey patch after pyzmq>=14.0.0
issue_body:
I found this problem when I have trouble interacting with ``zerorpc``, and I submit a issue in ``zerorpc`` before. Link: https://github.com/0rpc/zerorpc-python/issues/123

When gevent monkey patch is patched with ``pyzmq`` version >= 14.0.0, then the process get frozen after ``Socket.send`` is called(in the zerorpc case, the greenlet switched and never switched back).

And recently, I found ``pyzmq`` bumps its version to 15.1.0, so I tried again, and the problem is still there.

So I take a look into ``zerorpc`` source code, and I found out that zerorpc takes a non-copy ``send``/``recv`` which triggers the issue. Here is a small demo: https://gist.github.com/faith0811/34ae622abf2560820ab7

The copy version works well, but the non-copy version is broken when monkey patch is applied.

With some digging, I found that the ``garbage-collector thread`` caused this problem. Here is the commit: https://github.com/zeromq/pyzmq/commit/47f7684f5378adb2787459f372df283818bde28b

I think, the ``nogil`` cython code calling monkey patched thread(which is greenlet) might breaks the event loop(not sure but likely).

After I reverted this commit, the code works well, but I cannot sure there is no other side affects.


thanks

issue_title: pub / sub vs. connect / bind unexpected behavior
issue_body:
I've read online multiple times now, that when setting up a PUB/SUB connection, the PUB should BIND and the SUB should CONNECT.

I tested the 4 cases: 
1) [PUB + connect - then - SUB + bind](https://gist.github.com/FirefoxMetzger/6d957681096a793b9e2cbc3acd1d6490)
2) [PUB + bind - then - SUB + connect](https://gist.github.com/FirefoxMetzger/c65c31a25889ff7e753cf53dae70954b)
3) [SUB + connect - then - PUB + bind](https://gist.github.com/FirefoxMetzger/8026d2d9b5ec97ef7f39844814d7ed06)
4) [SUB + bind - then - PUB + connect](https://gist.github.com/FirefoxMetzger/6910980f286e760e123f6fd531a041e0)

And case 1) - 3) deliver the messages as expected.

However, case 4) does not; instead the subscriber didn't receive anything. What is a bit unexpected, is that this [can be fixed](https://gist.github.com/FirefoxMetzger/883501b05120069f5fb6eb51ca012942) by adding a random `poller.poll()` before the messages are sent. The poll has to happen _before_ the publisher sends messages and _after_ the publisher has connected for this to work.

For me this is unexpected, but maybe someone more experienced has an explanation?
Also, I am not sure if this is pyzmq only or something related to libzmq.

issue_title: Duplicate Checkpoint Saver?
issue_body:
In DeepSpeech.py a `CheckpointSaverHook` is [defined](https://github.com/mozilla/DeepSpeech/blob/master/DeepSpeech.py#L1552) before the session begins:

```python
    # Hook wih number of checkpoint files to save in checkpoint_dir
    if FLAGS.train and FLAGS.max_to_keep > 0:
        saver = tf.train.Saver(max_to_keep=FLAGS.max_to_keep)
        hooks.append(tf.train.CheckpointSaverHook(checkpoint_dir=FLAGS.checkpoint_dir, save_secs=FLAGS.checkpoint_secs, saver=saver))
```

Both this hook and the checkpoint parameters are [passed](https://github.com/mozilla/DeepSpeech/blob/master/DeepSpeech.py#L1552) to the `MonitoredTrainingSession` - is this an unintentional duplication?

```python
        with tf.train.MonitoredTrainingSession(...
                                               hooks=hooks,
                                               checkpoint_dir=FLAGS.checkpoint_dir,
                                               save_checkpoint_secs=FLAGS.checkpoint_secs if FLAGS.train else None,
                                               ...) as session:
```

issue_title: SIBImporter ReadString causes vector subscript out-of-bounds
issue_body:
When running the unit tests in Debug mode on MSVC, I get a vector subscript out-of-bounds error.
This is caused by a very silly line of code inside of the ReadString function inside of SIBImporter.cpp:
str[str.size()] = '\0';
I believe the process of converting this function to using std::vector over manually allocated buffers caused this.
Enough space is reserved in 'str' for this to never surface as a bug in production, but it is very much a bug.

issue_title: Newline issues on Windows
issue_body:
Hi there,

Thanks for the excellent work you've done on the language server! Overall, it's working great on OSX and Linux - I'm working on incorporating it into Oni: https://github.com/extr0py/oni

There is one blocking issue on Windows, however:

**Issue:** The LSP protocol expects there to be `\r\n\r\n` following the Content-Header. This works as expected on OSX and Linux. However, on Windows, we actually get `\r\r\n\r\r\n`, which some LSP clients will not handle this. `vscode-jsonrpc` is strict on matching this and therefore never realizes the stream is complete, and never completes initialization. I've tested this using the _stdio_ strategy - I haven't validated with the _tcp_ strategy.

**Defect:** It turns out Python has some 'magic' behavior with newlines - see https://stackoverflow.com/questions/2536545/how-to-write-unix-end-of-line-characters-in-windows-using-python. It looks like Python is converting `\n` on Windows to `os.linesep` - which is `\r\n`. 

This is the impacted code (`server.py`):
```
    def _write_message(self, msg):
        body = json.dumps(msg, separators=(",", ":"))
        content_length = len(body)
        response = (
            "Content-Length: {}\r\n"
            "Content-Type: application/vscode-jsonrpc; charset=utf8\r\n\r\n"
            "{}".format(content_length, body)
        )
        self.wfile.write(response)
        self.wfile.flush()
```

In this case, the `\n` in the `\r\n` is getting expanded - so we actually end up with `\r\n`.

**Proposed Fix**: The `os.linesep` should be checked. If it is `\n`, we should use `\r\n` as the line ending to conform to the protocol. If it is `\r\n`, that means `\n` will be expanded to `\r\n`, so we should use `\n` as the terminator above. 

I'm not an expert in Python, so there may be a cleaner way to fix this. It looks like when reading from a file, there are options in terms of handling newlines, so maybe there is an option to set when writing to the output. I'm not sure as well if this would cause problems with the tcp server.

Let me know if this isn't clear - happy to give more info.

issue_title: Docker container does not work due to Python v3 incompatibility
issue_body:
I have successfully built a Docker container from Dockerfile.dev using tag 19.2.1, but "vca" command does not work in the container. This is because the container is built based on "python:3", but pyvcloud module is not compatible with Python v3 due to StringIO module whose usage has changed in Python 3.0, see for example https://stackoverflow.com/questions/11914472/stringio-in-python3

So either the container needs to be modified to use Python v2, or pyvcloud made compatible with Python v3.

issue_title: gpgsig not populated correctly
issue_body:
Unlike the other attributes in a Commit object, the attribute [`gpgsig` is always set by the `Commit` constructor](https://github.com/gitpython-developers/GitPython/blob/df5095c16894e6f4da814302349e8e32f84c8c13/git/objects/commit.py#L133
). If you look at all the previous lines, they are guarded with a `if X is not None:`.

Since the Commit object can then be created with `gpgsig = None`, this causes the [`__getattr__ in LazyMixin`](https://github.com/gitpython-developers/gitdb/blob/13ad9b1199331a35e23f65c735acf482be09eae3/gitdb/util.py#L232) to not be called, which is responsible for calling [`_set_cache_`](https://github.com/gitpython-developers/GitPython/blob/df5095c16894e6f4da814302349e8e32f84c8c13/git/objects/commit.py#L139) which ultimately calls [`_deserialize` to populate `gpgsig`](https://github.com/gitpython-developers/GitPython/blob/df5095c16894e6f4da814302349e8e32f84c8c13/git/objects/commit.py#L496).

This unfortunately causes the bug that if `gpgsig` is the _first_ attribute read, it returns `None` even when the commit is signed. For example, if before reading `gpgsig`, we read another attribute like `message`, reading `gpgsig` will work. This made the bug hard to discover and debug. :disappointed:

I believe that this bug can be fixed just by adding a `if gpgsig is not None:` conditional in the line above.

issue_title: Pin `pipenv install X` to compatible version of X in Pipfile instead of *
issue_body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

In my experience, `pipenv` seems to be a bit too eager to update to newer versions of dependencies (see e.g. #1219). Based on other tools like `cargo` and `npm`, I would expect `pipenv install` in a newly checked out project to get me a working environment for running the project. However, since `pipenv install X` pins to `X = "*"` in the Pipfile, it is possible that I'll get newer versions of the deps incompatible with the given project.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

See also <https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/issues/1219#issuecomment-360690738>:

> Maybe Pipenv should do what npm does and make `pipenv install` pin to the major version instead of `'*'`.

I think it would be great if `pipenv install X` pinned to `X = "~=<current_version>"` instead of `X = "*"`, ideally by default (but I admit my perspective may be limited by my particular use cases) or at least through an option that can be specified on the command line and/or in the Pipfile itself, something like `--pin-to-current` / `pin_to_current = true`.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

What I'm doing currently trying to remember to run `pipenv install --ignore-pipfile` instead of `pipenv install` when setting up a project for development on a new machine. However, this skips all updates, even compatible ones (bug / security fixes etc.), since the purpose of `--ignore-pipfile` is deterministic builds for production, not setting up a compatible environment for development.

issue_title: js-yaml doesn't seem to like map keys with line breaks
issue_body:
According to yamllint.com (and to the spec as far as I understand it) this is valid YAML:
```
foo
bar: |
  baz
```
The js-yaml online demo can't parse that however. 
```
JS-YAML: end of the stream or a document separator is expected at line 2, column 4:
    bar: |
       ^
```
Something like the following is where I'm coming from:
```
- really very long text for a map key where one could reasonably expect 
  a line-break:
    - some more stuff
    - and yet more stuff which is also very long-winded and requires a
      line-break
```
which generates:
```
JS-YAML: bad indentation of a sequence entry at line 2, column 15:
      a line-break:
                  ^
```
Note that the line break within the list item is parsed correctly:
```
- really very long text for a map key where one could reasonably expect a line-break:
    - some more stuff
    - and yet more stuff which is also very long-winded and requires a
      line-break
```
```
[ { 'really very long text for a map key where one could reasonably expect a line-break': 
     [ 'some more stuff',
       'and yet more stuff which is also very long-winded and requires a line-break' ] } ]
```

issue_body:
In 0.0.1 if auth options are set, specifying how to connect to kubernetes, the default config `~/.kube/config` (or the one specified in `KUBECONFIG`) was picked up and used.

The commit 13d5d86ca89035b9f596032e7a34f3cc33bf8f18 broke this. The only way to use a config file now is to explicitly specify one in `kubeconfig`.

---

I guess the proper way to handle this in [KubernetesObjectHelper.client_from_config](https://github.com/openshift/openshift-restclient-python/blob/master/openshift/helper/kubernetes.py#L18) is:

- If `config_file` is not set, and a default config file does not exist, then `return ApiClient(config=ConfigurationObject())`

This is how [load_kube_config](https://github.com/kubernetes-client/python-base/blob/824c03c7eee71dd5ac52fada8d7d36aecf81a781/config/kube_config.py#L333) determines which file to use:

``` python
KUBE_CONFIG_DEFAULT_LOCATION = os.environ.get('KUBECONFIG', '~/.kube/config')

if config_file is None:
  config_file = os.path.expanduser(KUBE_CONFIG_DEFAULT_LOCATION)
```

We now have to decide whether to just fix this, or wait and warn, or not fix - see : https://github.com/scipy/scipy/issues/1547#issuecomment-171029805

Do y'all have any views?

issue_title: Installing headers
issue_body:
Installing headers seems to present a problem.

Let's assume:

```python
headers = ["src/cysignals/struct_signals.h",
           "src/cysignals/debug.h",
           "src/cysignals/macros.h",
           "src/cysignals/pxi.h",
           "build/src/cysignals/signals_api.h",
           "build/src/cysignals/signals.h"]
```

## headers

We could use `setup(headers=headers)` as intended. However, because of pypa/virtualenv#510 this will fail in virtualenvs, e.g. on travis-ci.

## package_data

We could try `setup(package_data={"cysignals": ["signals.pxi", "signals.pxd"] + headers}` but this will fail because the arguments to `package_data` must relative to the source directory of said module. We switched to cythonizing in the build directory rather than the source directory in 338d8e433a3b3ecaeed46c7c1925c98d45ff3762 which means some of our headers are not in the source directory. 

## data_files

We could try `setup(data_files = [(os.path.join(sys.prefix, "include"), headers)])` but that produces problems for distributions as pointed out by @kiwifb at 89dc7a7235acf9f5a7a4b4b37b68b15d0c0d350f

issue_title: RFC Remove or keep "Remember me" feature?
issue_body:
To remember me or not to remember me that's the question.

Currently the "Remember me" together with the session activity tracking is broken:

E.g. you have two sessions with remember me open, then from session 1 you revoke the session 2. What will happen when the browser window with session 2 is reload is that the user is logged in via the remember me token (also when a user is logged in via remember me, the session activity tracking is not created). This should not happen since you revoked the session (when you logout, the remember me token is removed)

Now the question is if we really need the remember me feature anymore? Currently we are using permanent sessions with lifetime of 31 days. I think the remember me feature is useful if you have short session lifetime (like minutes or hours), but probably not when you have long session life times.

**Proposal 1: Remove remember me**
This will solve all problems with remember me loopholes. 

**Proposal 2: Keep remember me**
We will need to monkey patch Flask-Security and Flask-Login to make it work with the session activity tracking.

Any strong opinions on either side?
@jacquerie @jmartinm This affects you related to your recent PR in oauthclient: inveniosoftware/invenio-oauthclient#134

issue_title: Custom html in README
issue_body:
I'm concerned at the nature of the custom html added to the README. As per my [review of the iris-grib README update](https://github.com/SciTools/iris-grib/pull/101#pullrequestreview-125614977), the changes have introduced accessibility problems and some questionable stylistic changes, arguably without adding anything beneficial to the README as was.

For the sake of having everything left-aligned, we could have an equivalent README written in pure markdown with no custom html. We should also drop the title-masquerading-as-hyperlink business and just include an explicit link to the cf-units docs in the README body - doing so will be much more accessible and clear.

Note: I do not disagree with updating the README to use markdown - that's definitely a positive step 👍

issue_title: Improve docstrings
issue_body:
There are two main issues with the docstrings as they are:

- [ ] They repeat themselves a lot. There should be one docstring per input per module, and all other functions should reference the "main" docstring. See [this line on `tears.py` on pyfolio](https://github.com/quantopian/pyfolio/blob/master/pyfolio/tears.py#L419) for an example.

- [ ] There aren't any examples. As good as verbose, detailed explanations of inputs are, an example would help illustrate a lot. See [this line on `tears.py` on pyfolio](https://github.com/quantopian/pyfolio/blob/master/pyfolio/tears.py#L104) for an example.

issue_title: INSTALL.md comments about numpy
issue_body: I'm finding that in the GalSim master branch, we use numpy.moveaxis, which first appeared in v1.11.  The INSTALL file says we regularly test on 1.11, but other versions may work.  I think we should explicitly say that numpy versions >=1.11 may work, but that versions below that definitely will not.  Please let me know if there are any objections to that change, otherwise I will make it on master.

issue_title: Add option for terse logging.
issue_body:
When using pip-accel for e.g. Travis-CI, the log output is much to long:
```
2015-10-19 23:50:55 testing-worker-linux-docker-357adccc-3199-linux-7 pip.req.req_set[3303] INFO Requirement already satisfied (use --upgrade to upgrade): wheel in /home/travis/virtualenv/python2.7.9/lib/python2.7/site-packages (from -r tests/requirements.txt (line 9))
2015-10-19 23:50:55 testing-worker-linux-docker-357adccc-3199-linux-7 pip.download[3303] INFO File was already downloaded /home/travis/.cache/pip-accel/sources/check_manifest-0.25-py2.py3-none-any.whl
```

Neither timestamp nor hostname, not even the process-id is meaning full here. Instead aligning the length of the logger names would ease reading the logs.

Please add an option for setting the log format, or at least for disabling timestamp and hostname. Another option would be to add an option to use the standard logging module instead of colorlogs.

(When thinking about this, the later option would a) quickly be implemented, b) allow configuring the logformat later c) reuse the pip-accel config-file for defining the loggers (see https://docs.python.org/2/howto/logging.html#configuring-logging)

issue_title: Django 2.0 support
issue_body:
I'm trying to run this project under Django 2.0.5 and there is a question -- is it supported?

For now I have running both targeted api and client with django 2.0. At first look targeted API works fine. But client api doesn't. In fact I have a problem -- function django.db.models.sql.compiler.SQLCompiler#execute_sql has different interface now:

```
def execute_sql(self, result_type=MULTI, chunked_fetch=False, chunk_size=GET_ITERATOR_CHUNK_SIZE):
```

but rest_models.backend.compiler.SQLCompiler#execute_sql has another:

```
def execute_sql(self, result_type=MULTI, chunked_fetch=False):
```

so when I try to execute any query for example MyModel.objects.get() it raises exception:

```
execute_sql() got an unexpected keyword argument 'chunk_size'

.../site-packages/django/db/models/query.py in __iter__
        def __iter__(self):
            <skipped>
            results = compiler.execute_sql(chunked_fetch=self.chunked_fetch, chunk_size=self.chunk_size)
```

I was able to fix this with adding **kwargs to SQLCompiler#execute_sql but i'm not sure if it works correct

```
def execute_sql(self, result_type=MULTI, chunked_fetch=False, **kwargs):
```

issue_title: Use the _W versions of the Windows API, especially for SEC_WINNT_AUTH_IDENTITY
issue_body:
It would be much prefered if you used the _W versions of the API, especially for the  auth identify.

Both usernames and credentials/passwords might contain UTF-16 chars outside of the current codepage.

In addition, your code reacts in funny ways if one changes the current user local (e.g. when using remote desktop, try switching between turkish, german and EN_US and use some umlauts or accents in the usernames or passwords, those might get silently converted).

Its close to trivial to use the UTF-16 APIs from python thanks to the usual unicode support.

issue_title: deserialize offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes
issue_body:
Should https://github.com/django-recurrence/django-recurrence/blob/master/recurrence/base.py#L992 not retrun the `datetime` based on `settings.USE_TZ`?  I'm working exclusively with offset-aware datetimes and when I call `recurrences.after(dt, inc=inc)` I run into `TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes`.

I propose replacing the following `return` line in `base.py` `def deserialize_tz()`
```py
return datetime.datetime(dt.year, dt.month, dt.day, dt.hour, dt.minute, dt.second`)
```
with
```py
        if not settings.USE_TZ:
            tzinfo = None

        return datetime.datetime(
            dt.year, dt.month, dt.day, dt.hour, dt.minute, dt.second, tzinfo=tzinfo)
```

The current implementation returns a offset-naive datetime after serializing/deserializing.
`python shell`
```py
from django.utils import timezone
import recurrence
today = timezone.now().replace(hour=0, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
dtstart = timezone.now().replace(hour=23, minute=59, second=59, microsecond=0)
rule = recurrence.Rule(recurrence.DAILY, count=1)
schedule = recurrence.Recurrence(dtstart=dtstart, rrules=(rule,))
[occurrence for occurrence in schedule.occurrences()]  # [datetime.datetime(2017, 11, 17, 23, 59, 59, tzinfo=<UTC>)]
serialized = recurrence.serialize(schedule)
deserialized = recurrence.deserialize(serialized)
[occurrence for occurrence in deserialized.occurrences()]  # [datetime.datetime(2017, 11, 17, 23, 59, 59)]
schedule.after(today, inc=True)  # datetime.datetime(2017, 11, 17, 23, 59, 59, tzinfo=<UTC>)
deserialized.after(today, inc=True)  # TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes
```

The expected behavior is a offset-aware datetime after serializing/deserializing.
```py
from django.utils import timezone
import recurrence
today = timezone.now().replace(hour=0, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
dtstart = timezone.now().replace(hour=23, minute=59, second=59, microsecond=0)
rule = recurrence.Rule(recurrence.DAILY, count=1)
schedule = recurrence.Recurrence(dtstart=dtstart, rrules=(rule,))
[occurrence for occurrence in schedule.occurrences()]  # [datetime.datetime(2017, 11, 17, 23, 59, 59, tzinfo=<UTC>)]
serialized = recurrence.serialize(schedule)
deserialized = recurrence.deserialize(serialized)
[occurrence for occurrence in deserialized.occurrences()]  # [datetime.datetime(2017, 11, 17, 23, 59, 59)]
schedule.after(today, inc=True)  # datetime.datetime(2017, 11, 17, 23, 59, 59, tzinfo=<UTC>)
deserialized.after(today, inc=True)  # datetime.datetime(2017, 11, 17, 23, 59, 59, tzinfo=<UTC>)
```

issue_title: DISCUSSION: Re-add dataset as wrapper for read/write options
issue_body:
Right now read and write methods are split between `Key` and `Entity`, requiring that the user explicitly specify things like `namespace`, `connection`, and once supported things like `eventual` and `force`, each time a rpc call is made. Instead, I would propose we use dataset as the main point of interaction between the user and the datastore (has mutation and fetch methods), and encapsulate all of the properties that users may not want to re-specify (connection, namespace, read/write options). I think this has a few advantages:

* Stylistically, things like namespace and connection should be only be specified once per object. While there is nothing wrong with the way it's set up now, I think that an entirely new object each time a new namespace or connection is specified will be more robust in the future.
* Makes it much easier for us to drop `Key` and/or `Entity` in the future as objects if we find a user-friendly way to do that
* `Transaction` `Batch` and `Dataset` can all share a common interface. I think this is a potentially huge improvement to usability. 

I'd love to hear thoughts on this.

issue_title: Spanner docs are outdated
issue_labels: [{'id': 539494117, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20spanner', 'name': 'api: spanner', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 77676134, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/documentation', 'name': 'documentation', 'color': 'bfdadc', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604244, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p1', 'name': 'priority: p1', 'color': 'ffa03e', 'default': False}, {'id': 486182624, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/release%20blocking', 'name': 'release blocking', 'color': 'ffa03e', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Spanner docs still talk about [checking out a session](https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/docs/spanner/snapshot-usage.rst) and then doing operations on that such as `session.snapshot` and `session.batch`. 
We changed the API long time back where such operations can be done directly on the database object and that is the API we want to promote. Please audit all the docs to scrub out all the calls directly made on session object and replace those with calls on Database object. This should be doe before Beta since we expect an uptick in usage once we mark the client as Beta and we want to people to use the preferred API.

issue_title: Setup codecov.io
RemoteRpc leaks http connections, eventually runs out so all requests block & timeout
issue_body:
If a Datastore object is created with DatastoreOptions that include HttpTransport whose HttpClient has set a org.apache.http.impl.conn.PoolingHttpClientConnectionManager, RemoteRpc soon exhausts all available connections.  Subsequent requests to the Datastore object block until the timeout expires and then fail.

The issue I believe is com.google.api.services.datastore.client.RemoteRpc does not call org.apache.http.conn.HttpClientConnectionManager.releaseConnection() to return a connection back to the connection manager and make it reusable by other consumers.

issue_title: IE breadcrumb display is ugly when using SASS version 3.4
issue_body:
When using the latest version of SASS (version 3.4) with bootstrap-sass, the breadcrumb separator is wrongly encoded as ISO-8859-1 when using Internet Explorer version 10 and 11.  

The problematic character seems to be the Unicode non-breaking space (\00a0) on this line:
https://github.com/twbs/bootstrap-sass/blob/master/assets/stylesheets/bootstrap/_breadcrumbs.scss#L17

In SASS version 3.3, the hexcode for the Unicode non-breaking space was left as-is in the final CSS. So, the final CSS would actually include "\00a0".  However, in SASS version 3.4, this behavior has changed, and in the final CSS the character is now translated to a Unicode space (" ").

While most browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Opera) properly display this new Unicode space (" ") from CSS, for some reason Internet Explorer wants to [read it as ISO-8859-1 and displays "Â " instead](http://stackoverflow.com/a/1462039/3750035). The resulting breadcrumb is something like this (assuming your breadcrumb-separator is the default slash):

    Home /Â  Page /Â  SubPage

In the end, this means that SASS version 3.4 + bootstrap-sass results in ugly breadcrumbs in Internet Explorer. Whereas, SASS version 3.3 + bootstrap-sass works perfectly in all browsers.

More information (including some possible workarounds using functions) on this new behavior in SASS version 3.4 can be found in this related SASS ticket/request: https://github.com/sass/sass/issues/1395

The only workaround I've found so far is to simply revert to using SASS version 3.3.

issue_title: discover() not using right interface
issue_body:
Following up on #615: SoCo often doesn't find the right interface to use for UPnP discovery. As a way of working around this, we already have the `interface_addr` parameter, with which one can provide the interface address to use. However, this is not a satisfactory solution: For example, it means that the code from the readme code doesn't work for some people.

The official controller sends the discovery packets on all interfaces. Unfortunately, python provides no cross-platform way to get all available interfaces. The documentation suggests using netifaces:

> Note:
> There is no easy cross-platform way to find out the addresses of the
> local machine's network interfaces. You might try the
> netifaces module <https://pypi.python.org/pypi/netifaces> and some
> code like this:
>
>      >>> from netifaces import interfaces, AF_INET, ifaddresses
>      >>> data = [ifaddresses(i) for i in interfaces()]
>      >>> [d[AF_INET][0]['addr'] for d in data if d.get(AF_INET)]
>      ['127.0.0.1', '192.168.1.20']
>
> This should provide you with a list of values to try for
> interface_addr if you are having trouble finding your Sonos devices.

Netifaces works with OS X, Linux and Windows, so it is expected to cover the needs of most users. We could use it to discover the interface addresses ourselves, if the system is supported. So, the question is:

**Can we add netifaces as a dependency?**

Pro: It provides a valuable cross-platform API we cannot replace
Con: Having more dependencies is generally a bad thing
Con: It is still in major version zero, so it might have bugs or break backward compatibility
--> so are we, and getting the interfaces is a core feature which has been tested well.

issue_title: Allow CDXJ piping from the indexer to replay
issue_body:
The current default behavior for the indexer is to print the resulting CDXJ to stdout. Ideally, this should be able to be piped to the replay script and used as a basis CDXJ instead of relying on an external resource. This would remove the step of needing to write to disk, and potentially speed up the process.

Though this can probably be done more elegantly through inter-process communication, a good first step might be just to allow:

`ipwb index myCaptures.warc | ipwb replay`

and have replay use stdin as a fallback if no other source for the archive index is provided (or maybe, even if one is).

issue_title: Regression in haproxy check since LP#1713165
issue_body:
The original bug reported in LP#1713165 [0] suggested that the following part of the haproxy check was a problem when there were backups of the config file present, as it recursively greps the haproxy config directory.

AUTH=$(grep -r "stats auth" /etc/haproxy | awk 'NR=1{print $4}')

The suggestion fix was -
AUTH=$(egrep "stats auth" /etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg | awk 'NR=1{print $3}')

The implemented fix was -
AUTH=$(egrep "stats auth" /etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg | awk 'NR=1{print $4}')
Note the awk token was not correct, and per comments on the original bug [1] and GitHub PR [2]

Despite this, this change has also made it into the OpenStack charms via a charmhelpers sync, which means we also have a regression in the Charms.

In addition to this, the fix also needs to be applied to 'check_haproxy_queue_depth.sh' in the same directory.

So far, I have found this has been merged into the following OpenStack charms, but I suspect that there are likely many more charms which would have synced the latest charmhelpers -
* ceilometer
* ceilometer-agent
* ceph-radosgw
* cinder
* cinder-backup
* cinder-ceph
* glance
* heat
* neutron-api
* neutron-gateway
* neutron-openvswitch
* nova-cloud-controller
* nova-compute
* openstack-dashboard
* rabbitmq-server
* swift-proxy
* swift-storage

The impact is that all of these charms install NRPE checks for haproxy, which report all backends as being down (which is a critical level alert) falsely due to not being able to grab status API credentials correctly.

I have also filed over at [3] but please kill with fire as appropriate, I know things have moved/are moving to GitHub.

[0] https://bugs.launchpad.net/charm-helpers/+bug/1713165
[1] https://bugs.launchpad.net/charm-helpers/+bug/1713165/comments/3

issue_title: data_iterator unnecessarily rebuilds the dataset on every epoch
issue_body:
data_iterator unnecessarily rebuilds the dataset on every epoch, which significantly slows things down.

`DataIterator. __call__` calls `DataIterator._yield_one_epoch` for every epoch. Each call of `DataIterator._yield_one_epoch` shuffles the input data, maps it to tensors and chops it into batches. In the non-lazy mode however, most of this work can be done only once before the first epoch, not before every epoch. 

In my application, about 55% of the training runtime is spent in the `DataIterator._yield_one_epoch` function. 

Assuming non-lazy mode, and assuming we don't want to reshuffle before every epoch, fixing this should be easy (call `DataIterator._create_batches` outside `DataIterator._yield_one_epoch`).

Got a little problem with the infer limit describe here : https://github.com/frictionlessdata/tableschema-py#tableinferlimit100-confidence075

issue_title: Can't flatten sub-sub-lists?
issue_body:
Let's say I have this list: `[[1, 2, 3, [4]], [5, 6, 7, [8, 9]]`

I want to extract this output: `[[1, 2, 3, 4], [5, 6, 7, 8, 9]]`

It seems like this query should work: `[*][]`, i.e., "project the input into a list, and flatten each element of the outer list" but it doesn't work. I get `[1, 2, 3, [4], 5, 6, 7, [8, 9]]`, which is the same as if I had just passed in `[]`. Oddly, `[*][0]` does return what I would expect, `[1, 5]` which the first element of each element of the outer list. Why is it that in the `[*][0]` expression the `[0]` operates on each element of the top-level list, while in `[*][]` the `[]` seems to operate on the list as a whole? I would expect that behavior out of `[*] | []`. Similarly, `[0][]` returns `[1, 2, 3, 4]` and `[1][]` returns `[5, 6, 7, 8, 9]`.

I see the same behavior both on the released 0.7.1 and the current develop branch.

Thanks!

--Joel

issue_body: dag_sizes in C implementation overflows with 32-bit size_t type. There might be other issues as well, see [cpp-ethereum issue](https://github.com/ethereum/cpp-ethereum/issues/1267)

issue_title: Add `get_next_log` and `fetch_candidate_head`
issue_body:
### What is wrong?
Add `get_next_log` and `fetch_candidate_head` described in https://github.com/ethereum/sharding/blob/develop/docs/doc.md#fetch-candidate-heads-in-reverse-sorted-order

### How can it be fixed
Implement `getNextLog` with web3.py(essentially `LogFilter.get_new_entries` acts like that) and `fetch_candidate_head` in `MainchainHandler`.

issue_title: id is not always a field on models, use pk instead
issue_body:
If the primary key of a model is not auto, then it should be accessed by `pk`:
https://github.com/djangonauts/django-hstore/blob/master/django_hstore/fields.py#L251

issue_title: itsdb key-table mapping is specific to official relations
issue_body: Now that there's a project ([XMT](https://github.com/goodmami/xmt)) that uses non-standard relations files for TSDB profiles, it's time to look at assumptions that crept into the code about the database structure. For starters, the `_primary_keys` data structure is not meant to be modified, but it clearly isn't applicable to the alternative DB structure. This affects how a database index is built, which affects row filters, etc.

issue_title: conda locking rudimentary and fragile
issue_body:
The conda lock is currently implemented using a two-step `glob()` / `open()` sequence. This has several issues:
* there is an inherent race condition: two processes could satisfy the `glob()` condition and take the lock at the same time
* the lock isn't cleaned up when a process terminates abruptly (which can happen when e.g. a CI job gets killed)

There are various ways to do robust file-based locking. Cross-platform Python libraries exist for this, e.g. [locket](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/locket/) or [py-filelock](https://filelock.readthedocs.io/en/latest/).

issue_title: conda create requires admin access on Windows in a multiuser installation
issue_body:
We are running Minoconda on a server machine for multiple users.
Miniconda is installed in C:\Miniconda3.
I set the permissions of the C:\Miniconda3 folder (and subfolders) such that all users have write access. Also explicitly for C:\Miniconda3\envs.
When doing a conda create -n p35 anaconda python=3.5 (also tried with python=2 and python=3), the system asks for admin rights for the executable pythonw.exe. This happens at the end, when all packages are downloaded and some linking needs to be done.
So users cannot create their own environments (so I am assuming that some temporary files are created or other files are written to other locations.
Would it be possible to make it so that users can create their own environments without needing an administrator password?

0.16.2

But what did surprise me was that conda then couldn't seem to find it:

issue_title: Channel Alias should add ending slash /
issue_body:
Channel Alias: http://conda.pydata.org/docs/install/central.html#channel-alias-channel-alias

Should add a trailing slash if there isn't one in the config file.  Otherwise it concatenates a nonsense string like:    https://conda.anaconda.org for `conda install wget -c bkreider` would become this =>   https://conda.anaconda.orgbkreider.

I don't see how it would ever be useful to not add the trailing slash there.

Does it have anything to do with the recommendation to create `environment.yml` by hand? Should I only include "top-level" packages and let `conda` figure out dependencies by itself? Or am I missing something obvious?
